brief_justification: Correctly identifies base rate dilution as the mechanism and confirms the arithmetic (0.4×3.0=1.2) matches observed metrics; traces first-order (yield drop) and second-order (sensitivity gain, radiation cost, LOS confounding) effects across the full tradeoff space; questions the LOS causal claim and flags radiation harm but does not explicitly name the reasoning approach or check for analytical blind spots; provides an explicit numerical verification that the two seemingly contradictory metrics are mathematically consistent; distinguishes causal claims (LOS) from correlational data and identifies what additional evidence would be needed (actionability of marginal findings); proposes a middle-ground policy alternative and draws the general screening analogy, but does not consider competing explanations for the paradox itself (e.g., reclassification of borderline findings); every step — from pre-test probability framing through arithmetic to committee decision criteria — is shown explicitly and reproducibly.
